encounter.reason: First pregnancy loss — vaginal bleeding and cramping at approximately 6 weeks gestation
encounter.location: Rocky Mountain Women's Health, Denver, CO

labs.name: human chorionic gonadotropin (beta-hCG), quantitative, serum
labs.note: Stat draw at initial urgent visit; lower than expected for 6 weeks gestation

labs.name: human chorionic gonadotropin (beta-hCG), quantitative, serum
labs.note: 48-hour follow-up draw; declining, confirming non-viable pregnancy

labs.name: human chorionic gonadotropin (beta-hCG), quantitative, serum
labs.note: Follow-up draw confirming complete resolution of pregnancy

conditions.name: spontaneous abortion (missed miscarriage), first trimester
conditions.note: Confirmed by declining serial beta-hCG levels and transvaginal ultrasound showing gestational sac without embryonic structures at 6 weeks gestation. First pregnancy loss. Complete resolution confirmed by beta-hCG <5 mIU/mL on 2024-06-14.

document_references.description: Documents the urgent office visit for first-trimester pregnancy loss at 6 weeks gestation, including transvaginal ultrasound findings, serial beta-hCG results confirming non-viable pregnancy, diagnosis of spontaneous abortion (missed miscarriage), expectant management plan, extensive patient counseling regarding prognosis for future pregnancies, and follow-up beta-hCG on 2024-06-14 confirming complete resolution.

diagnostic_reports.description: Gestational sac identified measuring consistent with 5 weeks and 6 days. No yolk sac or fetal pole visualized. No adnexal masses. No free fluid in pelvis. Findings concerning for early pregnancy failure.
